mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution designed for developers and edge computing. It is ideal for local development and CI/CD pipelines due to its simplicity and minimal resource requirements. However, it may not be suitable for large-scale production environments due to its single-node architecture.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using Snap, which is the recommended method for installation.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> This command installs the latest stable version of MicroK8s.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
This step allows your user to run MicroK8s commands without sudo by adding it to the microk8s group.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

```

> Replace $USER with your username if needed. Log out and back in for changes to take effect.

steps.title: Verify MicroK8s Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if MicroK8s is running correctly and lists the nodes in the cluster.

```bash

microk8s status

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> This command shows the status of MicroK8s and enabled add-ons.
> This command lists the nodes in your MicroK8s cluster.

steps.title: Enable Add-ons
steps.description:
This step enables essential add-ons like DNS and the Kubernetes dashboard for better functionality.

```bash

microk8s enable dns

microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables the DNS add-on for service discovery.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard for UI management.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step removes MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources and configurations.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

> This command uninstalls MicroK8s and all associated data.

uninstall.title: Remove User from MicroK8s Group
uninstall.description:
This step removes your user from the microk8s group to prevent future access.

```bash

sudo gpasswd -d $USER microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your username if needed.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations. This step upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.

```bash

microk8s stop

sudo snap refresh microk8s

microk8s start

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Stops the MicroK8s service before upgrading.
> Upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version available.
> Starts the MicroK8s service after the upgrade.
> Verifies that the nodes are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s service may not be started.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s start' to start the service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command is not linked to your PATH.

**Fix:**
Use 'sudo microk8s kubectl' or add an alias in your shell configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig may not be set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s kubectl config view --raw > $HOME/.kube/config' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s certificates may be expired or invalid.

**Fix:**
Restart MicroK8s with 'microk8s stop' followed by 'microk8s start'.

versionNotes.changes: Updated to Kubernetes v1.31, added support for new features like PodSecurity admission.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for certain legacy APIs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Run 'microk8s stop' before upgrading and 'microk8s start' after.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' when running 'microk8s kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a compatible Linux distribution and Snap installed.
